context_guatemala.text:
President Jacobo Árbenz won Guatemala's 1950 election by a wide margin. His agrarian reform, Decree 900, redistributes uncultivated land from large estates — targeting United Fruit Company above all others.

The United Fruit Company is not merely a corporation. Its board of directors includes former Secretary of State John Foster Dulles and his brother, CIA Director Allen Dulles. UFCO calls Árbenz a Communist. The CIA has prepared Operation PBSUCCESS: a covert program to arm, train, and insert a rebel force under Colonel Castillo Armas.

The plan: arm a rebel force in neighboring Honduras, conduct psychological warfare via CIA-run radio stations, provide air cover with U.S. pilots flying unmarked planes, and demoralize the Guatemalan military until Árbenz resigns.

What the CIA briefing does not mention: Árbenz is not a Communist. He has proposed to compensate United Fruit for the expropriated land at its own declared tax value — the company inflated its value after the fact for the compensation claim.

What the briefing doesn't say: Secretary of State John Foster Dulles was UFCO's former corporate lawyer. CIA Director Allen Dulles sat on UFCO's board. The land reform that Árbenz proposed — Decree 900, enacted in 1952 — expropriated idle UFCO lands (paying the company's own declared tax value as compensation). UFCO had declared that land was worth $627,000. When expropriated, UFCO suddenly claimed it was worth $16 million. Historians Stephen Schlesinger and Stephen Kinzer documented this in Bitter Fruit (1982): the operation to remove Árbenz cannot be separated from the Dulles brothers' personal and professional ties to United Fruit.

newspaper.headline: ARBENZ RESIGNS; GUATEMALAN PRESIDENT YIELDS TO REBEL PRESSURE
newspaper.bodyText: President Jacobo Árbenz Guzmán has announced his resignation and surrender of power to a military junta. Rebel forces backed by the army of liberation have secured the capital. The ten-year Guatemalan democratic "spring" ends.

newspaper.headline: CHILEAN MILITARY OVERTHROWS ALLENDE; JUNTA IN CONTROL
newspaper.bodyText: President Salvador Allende died today as the Chilean military seized power. The junta announced a "state of siege." General Augusto Pinochet leads the new military government. Thousands are reported detained at the National Stadium.
